[[BLOCK:slide0_shape1]]
Static and Semi-Dynamic Visualization

HAWAII PACIFIC UNIVERSITY

[[BLOCK:slide1_shape1]]
· In some software applications, such as IBM SPSS Statistics, the output is static and frozen.  
· No further manipulation can be made in the output after you hit the OK button.

[[BLOCK:slide1_shape2]]
Static visualization in SPSS

· In Python, there is some degree of freedom for manipulation.  
· pip install sweetviz  
· import sweetviz as sV  
· import pandas as pd  
· df = pd.read_csv('/Volumes/Extreme SSD/data_science_book/datasets/Chapter6/Diabetes.csv')  
· my_report = sv.analyze(df)

[[BLOCK:slide3_shape1]]
· Rguroo is a graphical user interface of R.  
· After a graph is created, you can still add more features to the graph.  
· However, it is only a one-way interaction, meaning you cannot directly manipulate the objects on the graph, such as linking the observations between the graph and the data table.

[[BLOCK:slide3_shape2]]
Semi-dynamic visualization in R

[[BLOCK:slide4_shape0]]
· The package "rgl" allows semi-dynamic data visualization using R.  
· However, it may be complicated to install and configure on certain operating systems (e.g., Mac OS).  
· No demo: This is for your information only.

[[BLOCK:slide4_shape1]]
Package 'rgl'  
September 26, 2022  
Version 0.110.2  
Title 3D Visualization Using OpenGL  
Depends R(>=3.3.0)  
Suggests MASS, rmarkdown, deldir(>=1.0-4), orientlib, lattice, misc3d, magick, plotrix(>=3.7-3), tripack, interp, alphashape3d, teltk, js(>=1.2), webshot2(>=0.1.0), downlit(>=0.4.0), pkgdown(>=2.0.0), extrafont, shiny, manipulateWidget(>=0.9.0), testhat, markdown, crostalk, V8, chromote  
Imports graphics, grDevices, stats, utils, htmlwidgets, htmltools, knitr(>=1.33), jsonlite(>=0.9.20), magritr, R6, base64enc, mime  
Enhances waldo  
Description Provides medium to high-level functions for 3D interactive graphics, including functions modeled on base graphics (plot3d, etc.) as well as functions for constructing representations of geometric objects (cube3d, etc.). Output may be on screen using OpenGL, or to various standard 3D file formats including WebGL, PLY, OBJ, STL as well as 2D image formats, including PNG, Postscript, SVG, PDF.  
License GPL  
URL https://github.com/dmurdoch/rgl, https://dmurdoch.github.io/rgl/  
SystemRequirements OpenGL and GLU Library (Required for display in R. See "Installing OpenGL support" in README.md. Not needed if only browser displays using rglwidget are wanted.), zlib (optional), libpng(>=1.2.9, optional), FreeType (optional), pandoc (>=1.14, needed for vignettes)  
BugReports https://github.com/dmurdoch/rgl/issues  
VignetteBuilder knitr, rmarkdown  
Biarch true  
RoxygenNote 7.20  
NeedsCompilation yes

[[BLOCK:slide4_shape2]]
Semi-dynamic visualization in R

[[BLOCK:slide6_shape1]]
Although you can use numbers (numeric output) to detect non-linearity, it is very complicated.  
Data set “orthogonal.sav” is used in SPSS Statistics.  
Demo only. No need to follow this counter-example.  
We want to detect whether the relationship between anxiety and performance is linear or nonlinear.

[[BLOCK:slide6_shape2]]
Why not numbers only?
